It would be helpful to add canonical 1D models of the solar chromosphere to SunPy. Models that would be helpful to include are those of [Vernazza, Avrett, and Loeser (1981)](https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/1981ApJS...45..635V/abstract) (especially VAL C) and [Avrett & Loeser (2008)](https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1086/523671/meta). There are probably other 1D chromospheric models that would be useful to have also.

These models are widely used in solar physics, but often have to be extracted from long tables in the papers.

### Proposed solution

PR will add a subpackage tentatively called `abstractions` which will include an [abstract base class](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html) tentatively called `AbstractChannel`. This PR is so we can iterate upon the design initiated by @wtbarnes.

This PR comes after a discussion about how [aiapy](https://gitlab.com/LMSAL_HUB/aia_hub/aiapy) and [xrtpy](https://github.com/HinodeXRT/xrtpy) can have a common interface for calculating effective areas and temperature response functions.  Having a common interface would be particularly useful for multi-instrument differential emission measures, and for cross-calibration between different instruments. This could potentially also be useful for other instrument packages like [irispy](https://gitlab.com/LMSAL_HUB/iris_hub/irispy-lmsal). 

This idea is a joint effort among @wtbarnes, @joyvelasquez, @nabobalis, @jslavin, and me.  

@nabobalis & @wtbarnes: since you're package maintainers (I think), please feel free to edit this directly.

Now that you mention it, I agree!

> Maybe we could have a `response` subpackage (where potentially some actual instrument responses could go) and then have `abstractions.py` under that?

{"action":"closed","number":15794,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/15794","id":1660141228,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAB_CCc5i876s","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/15794","diff_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/15794.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/15794.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/15794","number":15794,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Add mypy configuration file that ignores existing errors on a per-file and per-error basis","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"### Description

This prototype PR adds a [mypy](https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) configuration file, `mypy.ini`, that ignores existing errors on a per-file and per-error basis. When I run `mypy .` in the top-level directory of this repo with this file, it passes using mypy v1.8.0!  🎉 🎂 🥳 🎈 🎆 🥦

The configuration file includes global settings near the top that can gradually be set to true in order to globally approach mypy's strict mode.

This PR does not add mypy to the suite of continuous integration tests, since doing so may require an Astropy Proposal for Enhancement (see https://github.com/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/92).

### Motivation

Currently Astropy has no means to check that type hint annotations are correct, but there has been discussion over the last year or two about doing so, especially in #15170. If we want to methodically add *accurate* type hint annotations to Astropy, two steps that we'll need to take are:

 1. Get a static type checker like mypy to pass on the repo, such as by ignoring existing errors 
 2. Add the static type checker to the suite of CI tests 

This PR is a *possible* path forward for the first step, but there are other alternatives.  

### Backstory

This PR came about because I wrote a short script for https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2424 that parses the output of mypy and creates the sections in `mypy.ini` to ignore existing errors on a per-file and per-error basis. Since it was pretty straightforward to run for Astropy too, I decided to submit a demo/prototype PR. 

### Consequences

 - This PR would give Astropy a mypy configuration that passes, which would enable contributors to make progress on typing certain portions of Astropy.
 - If mypy is added to CI after merging this PR, then we could ensure that each existing module would not get any new categories of typing errors. Similarly, we could ensure that new files would not have any unintended typing errors.
 - The fine-grained approach would let maintainers go file-by-file and error-by-error to improve typing in Astropy.
 - The sections specifying the per-file and per-error ignores were created mostly automatically which a script that I plan to share. It would require extra care to mix manual configuration settings on a per-subpackage or per-module basis.
 - The per-file configurations would be annoying to manage manually due to its size. Since mypy is still changing over time, new errors could crop up that would need to be added.

### Related issues

Closes #12970. This is similar to #12971, but this PR takes a much more fine-grained approach.

Description

This prototype PR adds a [mypy](https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) configuration file, `mypy.ini`, that ignores existing errors on a per-file and per-error basis. When I run `mypy .` in the top-level directory of this repo with this file, it passes using mypy v1.8.0!  🎉 🎂 🥳 🎈 🎆 🥦

The configuration file includes global settings near the top that can gradually be set to true in order to globally approach mypy's strict mode.

This PR does not add mypy to the suite of continuous integration tests, since doing so may require an Astropy Proposal for Enhancement (see https://github.com/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/92).

### Motivation

Currently Astropy has no means to check that type hint annotations are correct, but there has been discussion over the last year or two about doing so, especially in #15170. If we want to methodically add *accurate* type hint annotations to Astropy, two steps that we'll need to take are:

 1. Get a static type checker like mypy to pass on the repo, such as by ignoring existing errors 
 2. Add the static type checker to the suite of CI tests 

This PR is a *possible* path forward for the first step, but there are other alternatives.  

### Backstory

This PR came about because I wrote a short script for https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2424 that parses the output of mypy and creates the sections in `mypy.ini` to ignore existing errors on a per-file and per-error basis. Since it was pretty straightforward to run for Astropy too, I decided to submit a demo/prototype PR. 

### Consequences

 - This PR would give Astropy a mypy configuration that passes, which would enable contributors to make progress on typing certain portions of Astropy.
 - If mypy is added to CI after merging this PR, then we could ensure that each existing module would not get any new categories of typing errors. Similarly, we could ensure that new files would not have any unintended typing errors.
 - The fine-grained approach would let maintainers go file-by-file and error-by-error to improve typing in Astropy.
 - The sections specifying the per-file and per-error ignores were created mostly automatically which a script that I plan to share. It would require extra care to mix manual configuration settings on a per-subpackage or per-module basis.
 - The per-file configurations would be annoying to manage manually due to its size. Since mypy is still changing over time, new errors could crop up that would need to be added.

### Related issues

Closes #12970. This is similar to #12971, but this PR takes a much more fine-grained approach.

The impacts we've noticed have been:

 - Editable installations needed to be redone (see [this comment](https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2598#issuecomment-2052430192)).
 - git does not remove directories, so it'll be necessary to manually remove the empty `plasmapy/` directory alongside the `src/plasmapy/` directory, and empty `tests/` subdirectories.
 - Having to change habits and get used to the new directory structure.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16412","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16412/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16412/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16412/events","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/16412","id":2284393501,"node_id":"I_kwDOAB_CCc6IKRQd","number":16412,"title":"[proposal] Switch from tox to nox for running continuous integration environments","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-07T23:37:09Z","updated_at":"2024-05-07T23:37:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Astropy uses [tox](https://tox.wiki) to run the environments for its continuous integration checks. While tox is in most ways a fantastic tool, its main drawback is that the configuration file ([`tox.ini`](https://github.com/astropy/astropy/blob/main/tox.ini)) is an [INI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file) file.  I personally find `tox.ini` files to be quite difficult to understand, modify, and maintain.

**[nox](https://nox.thea.codes/) is a project that is similar to tox, except that the configuration file is written in Python.** 🐍 I'm in the process of switching PlasmaPy's tox environments to nox sessions. I've been finding it _significantly_ easier to set up and understand sessions in `noxfile.py` compared to `tox.ini`.  I propose that Astropy switches its test runner from tox to nox.

For some background, I highly recommend [Hynek Schlawack's blog post on "Why I Like Nox"](https://hynek.me/articles/why-i-like-nox/), and trying out nox for new or personal projects.

## Example `noxfile.py`

```python
import nox

@nox.session
def tests(session): 
    """Run tests with pytest."""
    session.install('pytest')
    session.run('pytest')
```

This file defines the `tests` session, which can be run with `nox -s tests`.  (Vocabulary difference: a tox _environment_ corresponds to a nox _session_.)

## Tradeoffs ⚖️

Here are some of the pros and cons of making the switch. 

### ✅ Reasons to switch to nox

 - The configuration file would be written in Python! 🎉🎂💖🎆🪩🥦🐍
 - Sessions would be more maintainable in the long term because the configuration file would be written in the language that Astropy contributors are most likely to understand. 🌌
 - Sessions could make use of Python functionality. 🐍
 - Sessions could be generated dynamically. 🌠
 - Astropy contributors would no longer need to understand the INI format, since the repository would contain no more `.ini` files. ✨

While tox is in most ways a fantastic tool, its main drawback is that the configuration file ([`tox.ini`](https://github.com/astropy/astropy/blob/main/tox.ini)) is an [INI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file) file.  I personally find `tox.ini` files to be quite difficult to understand, modify, and maintain.

**[nox](https://nox.thea.codes/) is a project that is similar to tox, except that the configuration file is written in Python.** 🐍 I'm in the process of switching PlasmaPy's tox environments to nox sessions. I've been finding it _significantly_ easier to set up and understand sessions in `noxfile.py` compared to `tox.ini`.  I propose that Astropy switches its test runner from tox to nox.

For some background, I highly recommend [Hynek Schlawack's blog post on "Why I Like Nox"](https://hynek.me/articles/why-i-like-nox/), and trying out nox for new or personal projects.

## Example `noxfile.py`

```python
import nox

@nox.session
def tests(session): 
    """Run tests with pytest."""
    session.install('pytest')
    session.run('pytest')
```

This file defines the `tests` session, which can be run with `nox -s tests`.  (Vocabulary difference: a tox _environment_ corresponds to a nox _session_.)

## Tradeoffs ⚖️

Here are some of the pros and cons of making the switch. 

### ✅ Reasons to switch to nox

 - The configuration file would be written in Python! 🎉🎂💖🎆🪩🥦🐍
 - Sessions would be more maintainable in the long term because the configuration file would be written in the language that Astropy contributors are most likely to understand. 🌌
 - Sessions could make use of Python functionality. 🐍
 - Sessions could be generated dynamically. 🌠
 - Astropy contributors would no longer need to understand the INI format, since the repository would contain no more `.ini` files. ✨
 - [nox is being actively developed](https://github.com/wntrblm/nox).
 - Writing a nox session tends to be quicker than writing a tox environment, in my experience. ⏱️ 
 - Switching to nox would likely save developer time in the long run while improving developer experience. ⌛ 

### ❌ Reasons to keep using tox

 - Switching to nox would require perhaps a week of someone's time. This would include converting tox environments to nox sessions, updating GitHub Actions, revising the relevant sections of the development guide, and resolving any unexpected issues that may arise.
 - Switching to nox would require having to fully understand the contents of `tox.ini`. 😐
 - tox is currently doing its job satisfactorily, with enough Astropy contributors able to maintain it. 👥 
 - tox is an actively developed, well-established, and widely used project.
 - Switching to nox could disrupt some user workflows.
 - Switching to nox would lead to differences with packages whose architecture is inspired by Astropy's.

## Suggested process for switching

I recommend taking an incremental process rather than doing a monolithic PR:

 - Convert a subset of existing tox environments into nox sessions in one PR (e.g., just the docs environments).
 - Update GitHub workflows to use the nox sessions in a follow-up PR
 - Repeat.

> > Sessions could make use of Python functionality.
> 
> Any ideas in particular ?

For PlasmaPy, I'm considering dynamically creating nox sessions that run the tests for each subpackage.  For example, doing `nox -s particles` would let me run the tests and doctests for `plasmapy.particles`.  

I'm currently switching over to a nox session that regenerates pinned requirements files used in CI.  It's really helpful to be able to use a `for` loop!  I'm also adding a mildly helpful function to generates the names of the requirements files, which I'm likely to reuse in testing environments.  This is in https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2664.

I've previously considered dynamically extracting the minimum requirements from `pyproject.toml` to run tests against minimum requirements, eliminating the need to define them in both `pyproject.toml` and `tox.ini`.  (This is no longer necessary, as `uv` has a [`--resolution=lowest-direct`](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv?tab=readme-ov-file#resolution-strategy) flag to do this for us.)

> My understanding of tox is that is already allows defining sessions by composition, which could be argued is the only kind of dynamism that we need. I'm personally worried that introducing more dynamism would inflate the maintenance burden, not reduce it.

I suspect that the amount that this effect would increase the maintenance burden would be more than offset by the reduction in maintenance burden by switching from an INI format to a Python API.  I'm curious how the maintenance burden was impacted in other projects that have made the switch from tox → nox.

> I don't think we want to alienate the ecosystem that bloomed from astropy, and there's been a consistent effort to keep workflows as similar as possible between repos. Moving astropy away from tox would likely mean that we'd need to help coordinated and affiliated packages do the same, and it sounds like a sizable effort.

Agreed; to me this is the most compelling of the reasons not to switch. As with everything in software engineering, there are tradeoffs! ⚖️ It's difficult to find a balance between consistency & stability of package infrastructure, and adoption of new tools that can improve developer experience. 

Thank you again!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/2101229462/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

With this PR, @namurphy's PRs for tox and CI can redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

Thank you for doing it, and to everyone else for their feedback!  The approach taken here is a much better idea than the prototype I experimented with in #15794... 😮‍💨

The mypy docs contain a guide on [using mypy with an existing codebase](https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/existing_code.html), which I've found to be incredibly helpful for PlasmaPy.  This PR implements two of the recommendations from this guide:

> - Make sure everyone runs mypy with the same options. ...
> - Make sure everyone type checks the same set of files. ...

[The guide also recommends adding mypy to CI as soon as possible, which made the process of adding type hints to PlasmaPy significantly easier!  But that's out-of-scope here and may be best discussed in https://github.com/astropy/astropy-APEs/pull/94, which I'm hoping to get back to one of these megaseconds... ⌚]","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/2067398058/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

With this PR, @namurphy's PRs for tox and CI can redo of #12971 now that we have `typing`!

This PR provides a ``mypy`` configuration where all folders are ignored.
As a sub-package adds type hints the module may be removed from the top ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`` that ignores all errors and sub-package specific settings added in a new ``[[tool.mypy.overrides]]``, below.

e.g.

```
    [[tool.mypy.overrides]]
    module="*/astropy/cosmology/*"
    ignore_errors = false
    ...
```

**Non-goals**

1. type-hint anything in this PR.
2. force any sub-package to start adding type hints
3. ~decide *where* type hints will be stored: in the code, or as type stubs in a separate directory.~ This is resolved.

**Goals**

have a quality setup that:

1.  allows any sub-package to start adding type hints. 
2. ~works for either type hint location.~ This is resolved

With this PR, @namurphy's PRs for tox and CI can Putting the `tox` config into `pyproject.toml` would have the `.ini` content in a string called `legacy_tox_ini`, and I'm not sure that `.ini` syntax highlighting or linters would work with all IDEs if it is embedded in a TOML file.  If we could get away from the `.ini` format (my least favorite part about using `tox`) and have the configuration in TOML instead, then it'd be much more tempting to put the `tox` configuration in `pyproject.toml`.  

[A side note: I've been considering switching PlasmaPy from `tox` to `nox` because the configuration for `nox` is written in Python (https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/1734).]","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/2070547985/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Description

In some cases it might be possible to make the annotations more informative for type checkers by introducing overloads, but it is not obvious that would make them more informative for humans, so I've kept them rather simple. The one case where I did use overloads was `quantity_asanyarray()`. It is better to have more detailed annotation for that because there is no other documentation. Note that in the absence of a docstring I couldn't really tell what the exact purpose of this function is supposed to be, so I simply wrote down the signatures it gets called with.

🎆 LGTM, with one minor suggestion.","commit_id":"f39ed6930cd4bcbfe967757622fa8c543dda5e19","submitted_at":"2024-04-22T18:43:16Z","state":"commented","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323#pullrequestreview-2015543333","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"html":{"href":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323#pullrequestreview-2015543333"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323"}}},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323","id":1835047399,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAB_CCc5tYJnn","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323","diff_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16323","number":16323,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add type annotations to `astropy/units/utils.py`","user":{"login":"eerovaher","id":25464611,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NDY0NjEx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25464611?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher","html_url":"https://github.com/eerovaher","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"### Description

In some cases it might be possible to make the annotations more informative for type checkers by introducing overloads, but it is not obvious that would make them more informative for humans, so I've kept them rather simple. The one case where I did use overloads was `quantity_asanyarray()`. It is better to have more detailed annotation for that because there is no other documentation. Note that in the absence of a docstring I couldn't really tell what the exact purpose of this function is supposed to be, so I simply wrote down the signatures it gets called with.

- [x] By checking this box, the PR author has requested that maintainers do **NOT** use the "Squash and Merge" button. Maintainers should respect this when possible; however, the final decision is at the discretion of the maintainer that merges the PR.

🎆 LGTM, with one minor suggestion.","commit_id":"f39ed6930cd4bcbfe967757622fa8c543dda5e19","submitted_at":"2024-04-22T18:43:16Z","state":"commented","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323#pullrequestreview-2015543333","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"html":{"href":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323#pullrequestreview-2015543333"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323"}}},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16323","id":1835047399,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAB_CCc5tYJnn","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323","diff_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16323.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16323","number":16323,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add type annotations to `astropy/units/utils.py`","user":{"login":"eerovaher","id":25464611,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NDY0NjEx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25464611?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher","html_url":"https://github.com/eerovaher","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/eerovaher/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"### Description

In some cases it might be possible to make the annotations more informative for type checkers by introducing overloads, but it is not obvious that would make them more informative for humans, so I've kept them rather simple. The one case where I did use overloads was `quantity_asanyarray()`. It is better to have more detailed annotation for that because there is no other documentation. Note that in the absence of a docstring I couldn't really tell what the exact purpose of this function is supposed to be, so I simply wrote down the signatures it gets called with.

- [x] By checking this box, the PR author has requested that maintainers do **NOT** use the "Squash and Merge" button. Maintainers should respect this when possible; however, the final decision is at the discretion of the maintainer that merges the PR.

This pull request is to address and fix #16107

There are probably places that this may actually break intended functionality, I have added the `noqa` label where I think it's breaking the expected behavior. But in general we should avoid creating mutable arguments in function definitions.

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16109/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16109/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1973599377","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16109#issuecomment-1973599377","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16109","id":1973599377,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc51oryR","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-01T17:31:16Z","updated_at":"2024-03-01T17:31:16Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'm very much in favor of enforcing ruff rule [B006](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/rules/mutable-argument-default/), so thank you for submitting this!  Here's my go-to example for why mutable default arguments should be avoided:

```python
>>> def f(l=[]):
...     l.append(len(l))
...     return l
... 
>>> f()
[0]
>>> f()
[0, 1]
```

Even if code is working now, there's a chance that when changing code, mutable default arguments can lead to weird behavior that can take a while to track down.  This weird behavior can sometimes happen in edge cases that we forget to test.

To exclude certain subpackages from the rule, it would be possible to set up [per-file ignores](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/settings/#lint_per-file-ignores) in the ruff configuration.  That way, it wouldn't be necessary to have `# noqa` comments.  (My personal preference would be to put `# noqa: B006` comments directly in the code, since they would serve as extra motivation to avoid mutable default arguments.)

If the mutability of lists isn't necessary within a function, changing the default argument to an empty tuple may be a viable option.  Tuples are generally more efficient than lists, so this may lead to a very slight performance improvement.

Since `astropy.units` would require special attention, it seems like it would be worth separating those changes off from this pull request.  I often try to split up pull requests like this into one pull request per subpackage.

PR will add an APE that proposes a path forward for enabling static type checking in Astropy's continuous integration suite.  If anyone is interested in being a co-author, please let me know!

The process would be more or less to:
 - Enable mypy in CI, but only check a single file at first
 - Gradually enable static type checking in one or two subpackages (`astropy.cosmology` and `astropy.units`?)
 - Add a section to the developer docs on type hint annotations and static type checking
 - Allow subpackage maintainers to gradually enable static type checking in more subpackages

It's currently in an early draft state, and might be best though of as being in the brainstorming stage 🧠 🌩️.  Big picture feedback would be especially appreciated!  In particular, we will need to address the really valid concerns and considerations that have been brought up in https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/15170.

I'm also hoping to avoid being over-prescriptive, so as to give us flexibility and avoid the need to amend this APE when ruff implements a full static type checker. 🙃 

Closes #92.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/94/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/94/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/comments/1973934174","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy-APEs/pull/94#issuecomment-1973934174","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy-APEs/issues/94","id":1973934174,"node_id":"IC_kwDOANf7CM51p9he","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-01T21:20:03Z","updated_at":"2024-03-01T21:20:03Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"It's going to be a while before I can get back to this PR, so if anyone else wants to work on this, please let me know!

A quick follow up to #16064
I found the 10 longest (non-slow, not parametrized) tests with `pytest astropy/ --timer-top-n 20` (with `pytest-timer`), which is also about the same set of tests that take
longer than a second on my machine (supposedly a pretty fast M2).
On my install, `pytest astropy` takes about 2'15 on main, and 1'55 with this branch, hence I claim that skipping those 10 tests (out of 24k) might save about 10% CI time.

Request","color":"bfdadc","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-05T18:49:01Z","updated_at":"2024-03-05T18:49:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### What is the problem this feature will solve?

It takes a long time to build Astropy's documentation from scratch. From [recent builds on Read the Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/astropy/builds/), it takes ∼1000 s.

One of the most important qualities of continuous integration testing is to get rapid feedback. One thing that has happened a lot to me over at PlasmaPy has been that I run into a cryptic Sphinx error, and then have to wait ∼500 s between iterations trying to get the reStructuredText syntax right.  (We have a saying over at PlasmaPy that "Sphinx rabbit holes often have dragons in them."  🐰 🕳️ 🐉)

### Describe the desired outcome

We could use the [GitHub Action for caching](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/caching-dependencies-to-speed-up-workflows) to cache a documentation build from `main`, as well as the Python environment used to build it.  This cache could be recreated daily.

We could then add a workflow to be run in CI that would rebuild the documentation from the cached build (using the `-j auto` flag to have the `sphinx-build` be done in parallel while auto-detecting the number of processes to use).  We could potentially reduce the documentation build time in CI to ≲ 3 min.

### Additional context

This probably should not be a required check, at least not at first. I suspect there may be some occasional situations where a rebuild might fail or succeed incorrectly, such as when adding a Sphinx extension or modifying the configuration file.  It might help to have a label for skipping the documentation cache, and/or for the cache key to use [`hashFiles`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions#hashfiles) on `conf.py` and maybe also `pyproject.toml`.  

I've been trying to do this in https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2553 by using a `tox` environment to do this, but it seems that it rebuilds the documentation build from scratch (though caching `.tox` between runs did speed things up by a minute or two).  It may be necessary to use `sphinx-build` directly rather than creating a `tox` environment for this.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Request","color":"bfdadc","default":false,"description":null},{"id":182098,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODIwOTg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/testing","name":"testing","color":"d4c5f9","default":false,"description":null},{"id":3345090,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMzQ1MDkw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/Docs","name":"Docs","color":"02d7e1","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-03-05T18:49:01Z","updated_at":"2024-03-05T19:43:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### What is the problem this feature will solve?

It takes a long time to build Astropy's documentation from scratch. From [recent builds on Read the Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/astropy/builds/), it takes ∼1000 s.

One of the most important qualities of continuous integration testing is to get rapid feedback. One thing that has happened a lot to me over at PlasmaPy has been that I run into a cryptic Sphinx error, and then have to wait ∼500 s between iterations trying to get the reStructuredText syntax right.  (We have a saying over at PlasmaPy that "Sphinx rabbit holes often have dragons in them."  🐰 🕳️ 🐉)

### Describe the desired outcome

We could use the [GitHub Action for caching](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/caching-dependencies-to-speed-up-workflows) to cache a documentation build from `main`, as well as the Python environment used to build it.  This cache could be recreated daily.

We could then add a workflow to be run in CI that would rebuild the documentation from the cached build (using the `-j auto` flag to have the `sphinx-build` be done in parallel while auto-detecting the number of processes to use).  We could potentially reduce the documentation build time in CI to ≲ 3 min.

### Additional context

This probably should not be a required check, at least not at first. I suspect there may be some occasional situations where a rebuild might fail or succeed incorrectly, such as when adding a Sphinx extension or modifying the configuration file.  It might help to have a label for skipping the documentation cache, and/or for the cache key to use [`hashFiles`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions#hashfiles) on `conf.py` and maybe also `pyproject.toml`.  

I've been trying to do this in https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2553 by using a `tox` environment to do this, but it seems that it rebuilds the documentation build from scratch (though caching `.tox` between runs did speed things up by a minute or two).  It may be necessary to use `sphinx-build` directly rather than creating a `tox` environment for this.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1979508320","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/16161#issuecomment-1979508320","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161","id":1979508320,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc51_OZg","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-05T19:43:10Z","updated_at":"2024-03-05T19:43:10Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Thank you for the feedback! {"action":"opened","number":16162,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16162","id":1757796487,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAB_CCc5oxdiH","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162","diff_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162","number":16162,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"DOC: Speed up documentation build by ∼20% by using `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Astropy presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149. presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149.  This was inspired by https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2277, https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2280, & https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2281.  There's some related discussion on how to speed up the documentation build in CI in #16161.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1979815539","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162#issuecomment-1979815539","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162","id":1979815539,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc52AZZz","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-05T23:35:11Z","updated_at":"2024-03-05T23:35:11Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> This is fantastic! presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149.  This was inspired by https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2277, https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2280, & https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2281. What is the problem this feature will solve?

Astropy is a very heavyweight dependency and is difficult to use in a serverless, space-constrained environment like AWS Lambda. When installed from wheels for Linux aarch64, the `astropy` directory in `site-packages` takes up 58 MB. Of that, the `astropy/**/tests` directories take up 20 MB.

### Describe the desired outcome

Move the test directories out of the installed Python package, or exclude them from packaging.

### Additional context

Something like...

```python
from setuptools import setup, find_packages

setup(..., packages=find_packages(exclude=["tests"]))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1982145388/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

However, when I invoke `pytest` from the CLI using either `pytest` (in the astropy repo root directory) or `pytest --pyargs astropy`, the tests run and pass without issue. 

I suspect this has to do with some interaction between `asdf` and `astropy` during the test collection process. For example the issue disappears if I manually uninstall `asdf-astropy` as this turns off all the `asdf` related tests in astropy, which only consist of the doctests here: https://github.com/astropy/astropy/blob/d32a06ad59188cde97b736806407e7c466dd11cb/docs/modeling/models.rst?plain=1#L945-L969
As in this case the doctests will be skipped if `asdf-astropy` is not installed.

What is the problem this feature will solve?

Astropy is a very heavyweight dependency and is difficult to use in a serverless, space-constrained environment like AWS Lambda. When installed from wheels for Linux aarch64, the `astropy` directory in `site-packages` takes up 58 MB. Of that, the `astropy/**/tests` directories take up 20 MB.

### Describe the desired outcome

Move the test directories out of the installed Python package, or exclude them from packaging.

presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149.  This was inspired by https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2277, https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2280, & https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2281.  There's some related discussion on how to speed up the documentation build in CI in #16161.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":2,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1982198740","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162#issuecomment-1982198740","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162","id":1982198740,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc52JfPU","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-07T01:57:07Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T01:57:07Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'm still figuring out moving the remaining contents of `common_links.txt` over. Add a CI check to rebuild documentation from a cached documentation build","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":181675,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODE2NzU=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/Feature%20Request","name":"Feature Request","color":"bfdadc","default":false,"description":null},{"id":182098,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODIwOTg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/testing","name":"testing","color":"d4c5f9","default":false,"description":null},{"id":3345090,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMzQ1MDkw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/labels/Docs","name":"Docs","color":"02d7e1","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-03-05T18:49:01Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T15:28:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### What is the problem this feature will solve?

It takes a long time to build Astropy's documentation from scratch. From [recent builds on Read the Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/astropy/builds/), it takes ∼1000 s.

One of the most important qualities of continuous integration testing is to get rapid feedback. One thing that has happened a lot to me over at PlasmaPy has been that I run into a cryptic Sphinx error, and then have to wait ∼500 s between iterations trying to get the reStructuredText syntax right.  (We have a saying over at PlasmaPy that "Sphinx rabbit holes often have dragons in them."  🐰 🕳️ 🐉)

### Describe the desired outcome

We could use the [GitHub Action for caching](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/caching-dependencies-to-speed-up-workflows) to cache a documentation build from `main`, as well as the Python environment used to build it.  This cache could be recreated daily.

We could then add a workflow to be run in CI that would rebuild the documentation from the cached build (using the `-j auto` flag to have the `sphinx-build` be done in parallel while auto-detecting the number of processes to use).  We could potentially reduce the documentation build time in CI to ≲ 3 min.

### Additional context

This probably should not be a required check, at least not at first. I suspect there may be some occasional situations where a rebuild might fail or succeed incorrectly, such as when adding a Sphinx extension or modifying the configuration file.  It might help to have a label for skipping the documentation cache, and/or for the cache key to use [`hashFiles`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions#hashfiles) on `conf.py` and maybe also `pyproject.toml`.  

I've been trying to do this in https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2553 by using a `tox` environment to do this, but it seems that it rebuilds the documentation build from scratch (though caching `.tox` between runs did speed things up by a minute or two).  It may be necessary to use `sphinx-build` directly rather than creating a `tox` environment for this.

Edited to add: Locally, it took my computer ∼7 minutes to do a fresh doc build for Astropy, and ∼2.4 min to do a repeat documentation build. Given your response, along with the scale and tradeoffs to consider for the RTD ecosystem, it makes much more sense how caching doc builds would be really difficult there!  

My inclination would be for Astropy's CI to include a quick rebuild of the docs on GitHub Actions, in addition to the much more reliable RTD build with a preview.  Being able to get rapid feedback is essential for CI, even if the feedback is partial.  GitHub Actions also lets workflows be run on two processors, which itself might be able to reduce fresh doc build times by ∼25-40% compared to a serial run.

`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Astropy presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149.  This was inspired by https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2277, https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2280, & https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2281.  There's some related discussion on how to speed up the documentation build in CI in What is the problem this feature will solve?

It takes a long time to build Astropy's documentation from scratch. From [recent builds on Read the Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/astropy/builds/), it takes ∼1000 s.

One of the most important qualities of continuous integration testing is to get rapid feedback. One thing that has happened a lot to me over at PlasmaPy has been that I run into a cryptic Sphinx error, and then have to wait ∼500 s between iterations trying to get the reStructuredText syntax right.  (We have a saying over at PlasmaPy that "Sphinx rabbit holes often have dragons in them."  🐰 🕳️ 🐉)

### Describe the desired outcome

We could use the [GitHub Action for caching](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/caching-dependencies-to-speed-up-workflows) to cache a documentation build from `main`, as well as the Python environment used to build it.  This cache could be recreated daily.

We could then add a workflow to be run in CI that would rebuild the documentation from the cached build (using the `-j auto` flag to have the `sphinx-build` be done in parallel while auto-detecting the number of processes to use).  We could potentially reduce the documentation build time in CI to ≲ 3 min.

### Additional context

This probably should not be a required check, at least not at first. I suspect there may be some occasional situations where a rebuild might fail or succeed incorrectly, such as when adding a Sphinx extension or modifying the configuration file.  It might help to have a label for skipping the documentation cache, and/or for the cache key to use [`hashFiles`](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions#hashfiles) on `conf.py` and maybe also `pyproject.toml`.  

I've been trying to do this in https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2553 by using a `tox` environment to do this, but it seems that it rebuilds the documentation build from scratch (though caching `.tox` between runs did speed things up by a minute or two).  It may be necessary to use `sphinx-build` directly rather than creating a `tox` environment for this.

Edited to add: Locally, it took my computer ∼7 minutes to do a fresh doc build for Astropy, and ∼2.4 min to do a repeat documentation build.  This was with `make html -j 2` for two processors, which is what each GitHub Action allows.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1983979716","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/16161#issuecomment-1983979716","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16161","id":1983979716,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc52QSDE","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-07T16:46:11Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T16:46:11Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I just did some timings on freshly building PlasmaPy's docs locally with `make html`, `make html -j 2`, `make html -j 12`. The biggest successes I've had with speeding up doc builds in CI for PlasmaPy have been caching `.tox` between runs so that the Python environment need not be recreated every time, and using sphinx-globalsubs (#16162).

What is the problem this feature will solve?

Astropy is a very heavyweight dependency and is difficult to use in a serverless, space-constrained environment like AWS Lambda. When installed from wheels for Linux aarch64, the `astropy` directory in `site-packages` takes up 58 MB. Of that, the `astropy/**/tests` directories take up 20 MB.

### Describe the desired outcome

Move the test directories out of the installed Python package, or exclude them from packaging.

"gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk","html_url":"https://github.com/mhvk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mhvk/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":87,"closed_issues":176,"state":"open","created_at":"2023-06-22T16:07:38Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T16:33:58Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":11,"created_at":"2024-03-05T22:34:39Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T17:17:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/pulls/16162","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162","diff_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Astropy presently uses `docs/common_links.txt` to store global [substitutions](https://docutils.sourceforge.io/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#substitution-references) that can be used throughout the documentation. The contents of this file are read in through `docs/conf.py` and then added to `rst_prolog`. Because `rst_prolog` gets added to the beginning of every reStructuredText file and docstring, a lengthy `rst_prolog` can add minutes to the documentation build (see #15149). 🐌 

The [`sphinxcontrib-globalsubs`](https://github.com/missinglinkelectronics/sphinxcontrib-globalsubs) extension allows us to create the `global_substitutions` dictionary in `docs/conf.py` to contain substitutions for use throughout the documentation, eliminating the need to prepend `rst_prolog` to files and docstrings.

A clean doc build prior to this PR locally took 7.2 min. After moving most of the common links to `global_substitutions` in `docs/conf.py`, a clean documentation build took 5.8 min.  The doc build was shortened by 20%. 🎆  For these timings, I used `make html -j 2` so that the documentation build was done on two processors. (These times do not include the creation of a Python environment.)

Because `sphinxcontrib-globalsubs` only works with substitutions, I had to convert some links in the docs to substitutions (i.e., `SciPy_` → `|SciPy|`).  

I still need to clean this up a bit & document this a bit better. There's still some content in `docs/common_links.txt` that will require a bit more work. I'd also like to define these substitutions in a separate file which can then get imported to `docs/conf.py`, but I had some trouble getting that to work, so I may do this in a follow-up PR.

Closes #15149.  This was inspired by https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2277, https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/issues/2280, & https://github.com/PlasmaPy/PlasmaPy/pull/2281.  There's some related discussion on how to speed up the documentation build in CI in #16161.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":2,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/1984040909","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/16162#issuecomment-1984040909","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16162","id":1984040909,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc52Qg_N","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-07T17:17:50Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T17:17:50Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Ready for review!
