Request","color":"bfdadc","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-06-22T16:43:44Z","updated_at":"2022-06-22T16:43:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When `astropy.units.quantity_input` is used for an `"unknown"` physical type, the error message currently does not give information about what the physical type should be.  As an example, let's define...
```python
import astropy.units as u

angle = u.get_physical_type("angle")

@u.quantity_input(x=angle)
def f(x):
    return x
```
Then the error message for a `Quantity` with the incorrect physical type will be...
```pycon
>>> f(1 * u.m)
astropy.units.core.UnitsError: Argument 'x' to function 'f' must be in units convertible to 'unknown'.
```
We should change the error message so that instead of `must be in units convertible to 'unknown'`, it should give a specific set of units that are compatible with the unknown physical type, perhaps something like `must be in units convertible to 1 / rad`.

Many thanks to @dhomeier for noticing this in the `#units` channel on Slack!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/13361/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/13361/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

standard","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR adds keyword-only parameters to the numpydoc style guide, which were added in [PEP 3102](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3102/).  This change would specify that `keyword-only` gets added next to the type information, like with `optional`.  This was suggested in #330.  Here are two examples:

```Python
def f(*, x, y=None):
    """
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : int, keyword-only
        ...
    y : int, keyword-only, optional
        ...
    """
    ...
```

Here are some alternatives that I considered:
 - *Specify that a parameter is keyword-only in the description of the parameter.*  Some examples.
   ```Python
   def f(*, x=None, y=None):
       """
       Parameters
       ----------
       x : int, optional
           Some number.  Keyword-only.
       y : int, optional
           Or it could be in parentheses (keyword-only).  
       """
   ```
   I didn't choose this approach since the `keyword-only` might be missed by someone who is quickly scanning through the parameters, and because the information about if something is keyword-only is closely related to the information about if a parameter is optional.
 - *Include a separate section for keyword-only parameters.*  I didn't do this because if a parameter is keyword-only and required, someone might miss it if it is in a different section.
 - *Do not include keyword-only parameters in the standard.* I didn't choose this approach because right now there are different practices for specifying keyword-only parameters.  If someone is expecting `keyword-only` to show up next to the type, then they would be more likely to miss it if it is in the parameter description.  Also, if someone wanted to create an automated tool to automatically link to a definition of `keyword-only`, having a standard to follow would make it easier to implement.

Closes #330.  I'm open to alternatives and edits, in case anyone has suggestions.  Many standard","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR adds keyword-only parameters to the numpydoc style guide, which were added in [PEP 3102](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3102/).  This change would specify that `keyword-only` gets added next to the type information, like with `optional`.  This was suggested in #330.  Here are two examples:

```Python
def f(*, x, y=None):
    """
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : int, keyword-only
        ...
    y : int, keyword-only, optional
        ...
    """
    ...
```

Here are some alternatives that I considered:
 - *Specify that a parameter is keyword-only in the description of the parameter.*  Some examples.
   ```Python
   def f(*, x=None, y=None):
       """
       Parameters
       ----------
       x : int, optional
           Some number.  Keyword-only.
       y : int, optional
           Or it could be in parentheses (keyword-only).  
       """
   ```
   I didn't choose this approach since the `keyword-only` might be missed by someone who is quickly scanning through the parameters, and because the information about if something is keyword-only is closely related to the information about if a parameter is optional.
 - *Include a separate section for keyword-only parameters.*  I didn't do this because if a parameter is keyword-only and required, someone might miss it if it is in a different section.
 - *Do not include keyword-only parameters in the standard.* I didn't choose this approach because right now there are different practices for specifying keyword-only parameters.  If someone is expecting `keyword-only` to show up next to the type, then they would be more likely to miss it if it is in the parameter description.  Also, if someone wanted to create an automated tool to automatically link to a definition of `keyword-only`, having a standard to follow would make it easier to implement.

Closes #330.  I'm open to alternatives and edits, in case anyone has suggestions.  Many include positional-only and keyword-only parameters in standard","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2022-03-02T17:19:45Z","updated_at":"2022-05-10T01:03:24Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/pulls/367","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR adds keyword-only parameters to the numpydoc style guide, which were added in [PEP 3102](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3102/).  This change would specify that `keyword-only` gets added next to the type information, like with `optional`.  This was suggested in #330.  Here are two examples:

```Python
def f(*, x, y=None):
    """
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : int, keyword-only
        ...
    y : int, keyword-only, optional
        ...
    """
    ...
```

Here are some alternatives that I considered:
 - *Specify that a parameter is keyword-only in the description of the parameter.*  Some examples.
   ```Python
   def f(*, x=None, y=None):
       """
       Parameters
       ----------
       x : int, optional
           Some number.  Keyword-only.
       y : int, optional
           Or it could be in parentheses (keyword-only).  
       """
   ```
   I didn't choose this approach since the `keyword-only` might be missed by someone who is quickly scanning through the parameters, and because the information about if something is keyword-only is closely related to the information about if a parameter is optional.
 - *Include a separate section for keyword-only parameters.*  I didn't do this because if a parameter is keyword-only and required, someone might miss it if it is in a different section.
 - *Do not include keyword-only parameters in the standard.* I didn't choose this approach because right now there are different practices for specifying keyword-only parameters.  If someone is expecting `keyword-only` to show up next to the type, then they would be more likely to miss it if it is in the parameter description.  Also, if someone wanted to create an automated tool to automatically link to a definition of `keyword-only`, having a standard to follow would make it easier to implement.

Closes #330.  I'm open to alternatives and edits, in case anyone has suggestions.  Many thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/comments/1121751669","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367#issuecomment-1121751669","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367","id":1121751669,"node_id":"IC_kwDOALGa2c5C3JJ1","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-05-10T01:03:24Z","updated_at":"2022-05-10T01:03:24Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I was thinking that "keyword-only" and "positional-only" should be treated the same as "optional", since they're used in the same place for similar reasons.  

I took a look at the code and if I'm understanding things right, `numpydoc_xref_ignore` defaults to an empty set, so at present it is necessary to include "optional" in `numpydoc_xref_ignore` in order for it to be ignored.

I put "keyword-only" and "positional-only" in the example for `numpydoc_xref_ignore` in the configuration section, and tentatively did the same thing in `doc/conf.py` too.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/comments/1121751669/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

standard","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-03-02T17:19:45Z","updated_at":"2022-03-02T17:56:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/pulls/367","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR adds keyword-only parameters to the numpydoc style guide, which were added in [PEP 3102](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3102/).  This change would specify that `keyword-only` gets added next to the type information, like with `optional`.  This was suggested in #330.  Here are two examples:

```Python
def f(*, x, y=None):
    """
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : int, keyword-only
        ...
    y : int, keyword-only, optional
        ...
    """
    ...
```

Here are some alternatives that I considered:
 - *Specify that a parameter is keyword-only in the description of the parameter.*  Some examples.
   ```Python
   def f(*, x=None, y=None):
       """
       Parameters
       ----------
       x : int, optional
           Some number.  Keyword-only.
       y : int, optional
           Or it could be in parentheses (keyword-only).  
       """
   ```
   I didn't choose this approach since the `keyword-only` might be missed by someone who is quickly scanning through the parameters, and because the information about if something is keyword-only is closely related to the information about if a parameter is optional.
 - *Include a separate section for keyword-only parameters.*  I didn't do this because if a parameter is keyword-only and required, someone might miss it if it is in a different section.
 - *Do not include keyword-only parameters in the standard.* I didn't choose this approach because right now there are different practices for specifying keyword-only parameters.  If someone is expecting `keyword-only` to show up next to the type, then they would be more likely to miss it if it is in the parameter description.  Also, if someone wanted to create an automated tool to automatically link to a definition of `keyword-only`, having a standard to follow would make it easier to implement.

Closes #330.  I'm open to alternatives and edits, in case anyone has suggestions.  Many thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/comments/1057213523","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/pull/367#issuecomment-1057213523","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpydoc/issues/367","id":1057213523,"node_id":"IC_kwDOALGa2c4_A8xT","user":{"login":"namurphy","id":8931994,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzE5OTQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8931994?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy","html_url":"https://github.com/namurphy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/namurphy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-03-02T17:56:07Z","updated_at":"2022-03-02T17:56:07Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Cool!     Parameters
```Python
    ----------

    """
```
